posts.title: The Square ψυχή
posts.blurb: From Simonides to Proclus: how the image of the square man (τετράγωνος ἄνευ ψόγου) became the square soul. A corpus-grounded survey across six centuries, from Aristotle's εὐδαίμων through Aspasius's square stones to the Pythagorean tetrad of cognitive faculties.

posts.title: What Aristotle attributes to Socrates, and what it means
posts.blurb: Aristotle credits Socrates with two things (inductive arguments and universal definition) and draws the single word (χωριστά) that separates Socrates from Plato. A close reading of Metaphysics 1078b17–31 with Alexander and the ancient commentators.
